Article Title: 'Trademark arrogance': Sonia Gandhi says Modi govt treated NEET protesters as 'enemies of nation'
Article Text:
Sonia Gandhi alleged that even students heading home were not spared as police used batons indiscriminately.

Sonia alleges 'utter impunity'

PM Modi vows stricter law

NEW DELHI: Congress leader Sonia Gandhi on Friday launched a sharp attack on the Narendra Modi government, accusing it of responding to students' demands for accountability and education reforms with "cowardice and wanton cruelty" instead of meaningful action.She described July 20, 2026, as "a day of infamy", alleging that the Delhi Police and Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) used lathis and tear gas to disperse peaceful demonstrators. She also claimed that many protesters were injured and that even students returning home were allegedly beaten.Sonia Gandhi alleged that numerous peaceful protesters were injured, and even students heading home were not spared, as police used batons indiscriminately."The nation has seen some of the most depressing visuals of youth whose bodies have been scarred by pellets, the use of which would undoubtedly have been approved by Union home minister Amit Shah himself," the ex-Congress chief wrote in an article titled 'An education system's collapse, young India's trauma', published in The Hindu.Gandhi said the government's handling of the ongoing student protests reflected its unwillingness to engage with the concerns of young people."The videos from that day are seared into our memories and have stirred our collective conscience. Only the government and its captive media are spinning stories around this spontaneous rising tide of protest. It is time to say loud and clear: Stop, these are our sons and daughters, our young men and women," Gandhi said.She said students across the country had been demanding accountability over examination paper leaks and broader reforms in the education system, but alleged that the Centre had chosen repression over dialogue."The student protests are a natural consequence of these interrelated forces: today's bleak education landscape, which evokes only hopelessness, and the Modi government's trademark arrogance and unwillingness to be accountable or constructive," Gandhi wrote.She further alleged that the government had weakened India's education system while acting with "utter impunity, supreme self-delusion, and abject insensitivity".According to Gandhi, it was the moral, political and constitutional responsibility of every public representative to stand with students and protect their future.Referring to the police action against protesters, she claimed the use of force was consistent with the government's previous response to public demonstrations."We all remember vividly how armed policemen and paramilitary forces ravaged university campuses during the Citizenship (Amendment) Act-National Register of Citizens protests in 2020. The unacceptable excesses against India's women wrestlers can never be erased from our memories," she said.Gandhi also criticised the police action during the recent protests over the alleged NEET paper leak and the state of the education system."Their demands have been straightforward and to the point: accountability from the Government of India and education reform. The Narendra Modi government has responded to their courage with cowardice and wanton cruelty, treating them not as inheritors of the future but as enemies of the nation," she said.Sonia's remarks came a day after Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that the Union Cabinet would discuss a Bill to strengthen the existing anti-cheating law with stricter penalties for those involved in examination paper leaks.In a recorded message released late on Thursday night, the Prime Minister said the proposed legislation would include provisions for fast-track courts and tougher punishment for offenders. He said the Bill would be finalised after Cabinet approval and introduced in Parliament when the second week of the Monsoon Session begins.Modi said the government had been working for the past two and a half months to address concerns arising from the alleged NEET paper leak. He added that the Centre had ensured that nearly 22 lakh students did not lose an academic year by conducting the re-examination and declaring the results.The Prime Minister's announcement came shortly after the government replaced the Education Secretary amid the continuing protests over the alleged paper leak.Amid ongoing protests, the government named animal husbandry secretary Naresh Pal Gangwar the new secretary for higher education, while TK Anil Kumar the new school education secretary. Incumbent Vineet Joshi, who had charge of both, moved to low-profile panchayati raj department.Officials said the Prime Minister's Office (PMO) had remained in close touch with the education ministry over issues related to the NEET re-test and the implementation of recommendations made by the K Radhakrishnan Committee on reforms to the National Testing Agency (NTA).
Article Summary: Sonia gandhi alleges that even students heading home were not spared as police used batons indiscriminately. She described july 20, 2026, as "a day of infamy", alleging that the delhi police and central armed police forces used lathis and tear gas to disperse peaceful demonstrators.

Article Title: 'Paper leaks are no ordinary matter': What PM Modi said in his midnight video message
Article Text:
PM Modi promises tougher action in midnight address

NEW DELHI: With CJP-led protests over NEET-UG 2026 examination irregularities showing no signs of easing, Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday said the government would take stricter action against paper leaks."Friends, I know that paper leaks are not an ordinary matter. For lakhs of students and their parents, it is extremely painful," PM Modi said in a self-recorded video posted on X.PM Modi said the Union Cabinet would consider a draft Bill proposing Fast-Track Courts and stringent punishment for offenders."In the last two and a half months since the paper leak incidents, several major steps have been taken. The culprits have been caught; they are currently in jail," he added.PM Modi highlighted that the government made every effort to ensure that the students did not lose an academic year."Our most important responsibility was to ensure that a whole year for the students is not wasted. It was very important to conduct the exams immediately. The government used its full strength to arrange exams for nearly 22 lakh students in the shortest possible time. And just five or six days ago, on the 19th, the results were also announced.News of the happiness of successful students is coming from all over the country. "PM Modi said that the government has decided to take stronger measures to tackle paper leaks."However, we are not among those who are satisfied with just that. And therefore, today I have issued directions to the departments to set up Fast-Track Courts. The departments worked tirelessly and, late last night, provided me with a draft. This draft, which includes provisions for Fast-Track Courts and stringent punishments, will be discussed in the Cabinet tomorrow," he added."After incorporating suggestions from Cabinet colleagues, it will be given a final shape. Efforts will be made to get this Bill passed in the House as soon as possible, as the second week of the Parliament session begins on Monday," he said.The PM also urged activist Sonam Wangchuk to follow doctors' advice, recover well and gain weight as soon as possible."I urge Sonam ji to follow his routine as per the doctors' advice and regain his old weight as soon as possible. I pray to the Lord that Sonam ji remains healthy," he said in a post on X.Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike on Friday at Medanta Hospital in the presence of Union ministers J P Nadda and Jitendra Singh, along with senior leaders of the Apex Body of Leh Ladakh.In a post on X, Wangchuk said he decided to call off the fast after prolonged negotiations on various conditions and in view of the possibility of violence in the country.The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), which has been spearheading the protest at Jantar Mantar, welcomed Wangchuk's decision but said its agitation would continue until Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.
Article Summary: Pm modi promises tougher action in midnight address on paper leaks. Union cabinet will consider a draft bill proposing fast-track courts and stringent punishment for offenders. Pm also urged activist sonam wangchuk to follow doctors' advice, recover well and gain weight as soon as possible.

Article Title: CJP, Centre set for 12:30 pm talks at Constitution Club; Pradhan's resignation among key demand
Article Text:
CJP-Centre meeting to be held today (ANI)

'Peaceful protest will continue'

NEW DELHI: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) delegation will meet Union ministers at the Constitution Club of India at 12.30 pm on Friday, after the Centre agreed to hold talks at a neutral venue—a key demand raised by the outfit during its ongoing agitation over the NEET row.The meeting comes a day after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike at Medanta Hospital in the presence of Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh, paving the way for formal discussions between the government and the protesters.Confirming the development, CJP chief spokesperson Saurav Das said the organisation welcomed the government's decision to shift the talks to a neutral venue."Our protest will continue; Constitution Club of India chosen as venue for meeting with government ministers, hoping for an open-minded dialogue," Das told reporters on Thursday.CJP spokesperson Vaishnavi Gaur said the delegation would place the same demands it has been raising since the protests began."We will be putting forward our demands, which we have been consistent with from the first day," she said.According to Gaur, the key demands include the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, compensation for the families affected by the NEET controversy, and withdrawal of all FIRs registered against what she described as "innocent protesters."Following Wangchuk's decision to end his fast, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke thanked him for what he called an extraordinary act of courage."We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days. Thank you for your extraordinary courage and sacrifice. By putting your own life on the line, you awakened the conscience of an entire nation. Your life is far too precious to this country," Dipke wrote in a post on X.Despite Wangchuk ending his fast, the CJP has maintained that its peaceful protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar will continue until its demands, including Pradhan's resignation, are addressed.
Article Summary: Cjp delegation will meet union ministers at the constitution club of india at 12.30 pm on friday. Meeting comes a day after activist sonam wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike. Cjp has maintained that its peaceful protest at delhi's jantar mantar will continue until its demands, including dharmendra pradhan's resignation, are addressed.

Article Title: BJP attacks oppn for not allowing House discussion on leaks
Article Text:
Nitin Nabin Nitin Nabin Nitin Nabin 1

2

NEW DELHI: BJP Thursday sought to turn the heat on Congress and its allies over the issue of paper leaks as its MPs raised slogans and carried placards in the Parliament complex, targeting Rahul Gandhi and opposition netas for not allowing a discussion on the issue despite govt's willingness to do so.BJP president Nitin Nabin accused Rahul of running away from debate, asserting that Congress feared that its double-standards would be exposed in Parliament if discussion happened, given that the Opposition had only misled and deceived the youth during its long stint in office."I dare them to a debate, but Rahul and his allies will not agree. The NDA govt has shaped the future of the youth in the right direction," he said, highlighting that India is now being recognised for its start-ups and unicorns. Congress looks at the youth as nothing but stone-pelters, he said, adding the young population is with PM Modi. Former minister Anurag Thakur asked the Opposition not to play petty politics with students' future. Instead of running away, they should participate in a debate in Parliament, he said, lauding Modi's announcement for setting up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those behind paper leaks. Another party MP, Sudhanshu Trivedi, said BJP and its allies are fully sensitive to the situation.
Article Summary: Bjp president nitin nabin accused rahul of running away from debate. He said congress feared that its double-standards would be exposed in parliament if discussion happened. Former minister anurag thakur asked the opposition not to play petty politics with students' future.

Article Title: Honeymoon murder case: SC cancels Sonam Raghuvanshi's bail, orders her to surrender in three weeks
Article Text:
The order came after the Meghalaya government challenged the Meghalaya high court's decision to grant Sonam bail.

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you agree with the decision to set aside Sonam Raghuvanshi's bail? Yes, it's fair No, she should remain free 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Thursday set aside the bail granted to Sonam Raghuvanshi, the prime accused in the alleged murder of her husband, Raja Raghuvanshi, during their honeymoon in Meghalaya in May 2025. The apex court directed her to surrender before the trial court within three weeks, PTI reported.“We are inclined to observe that continued enlargement on bail of the respondent (Sonam Raghuvanshi) at this stage might hinder the ongoing trial. In such a matter we are inclined to set-aside the bail order... The respondent has been given two weeks to surrender. In the event of trial not concluding within six months the respondent can submit an application seeking bail,” the Court observed, as quoted by news agency ANI.The order came after the Meghalaya government challenged the Meghalaya high court's decision to grant Sonam bail. The high court had upheld the trial court's order, holding that she was entitled to bail because the mandatory "grounds of arrest" had not been furnished to her at the time of arrest.During an earlier hearing, a bench of Justices MM Sundresh and PB Varale had offered Sonam two options: Either surrender to facilitate the examination of public witnesses while the appeal against her bail was pending, or contest the Meghalaya government's appeal on merits."You (Sonam's counsel) have two options. Either we will pass an order on merits or we will ask you to surrender, let the public witnesses question you - meanwhile we will decide the matter on merits (on bail)," the bench had observed. The judges had also advised her counsel that "surrender" would be the better course.Opposing the bail, Solicitor General Tushar Mehta, appearing for the Meghalaya government, argued that Sonam had voluntarily surrendered before the police and therefore could not later claim that the grounds of arrest had not been provided. He also maintained that the error in the arrest memo was merely typographical, with Section 403(1) being mentioned instead of Section 103(1) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, which deals with murder.
Article Summary: The supreme court on thursday set aside the bail granted to sonam raghuvanshi. The apex court directed her to surrender before the trial court within three weeks. The order came after the meghalaya high court's decision to grant sonam bail.

Article Title: Govt reshuffles top bureaucrats in ministries: Education secretary shifted out
Article Text:
Dr. Vineet Joshi replaced as education secretary

NEW DELHI: The Centre on Thursday carried out a major bureaucratic reshuffle, effecting 13 secretary-level appointments across ministries and departments, including key changes in the education ministry, amid continuing protests over the alleged NEET paper leak. Senior IAS officer Naresh Pal Gangwar has been appointed as secretary of the department of higher education and T K Anil Kumar as secretary of the department of school education and literacy, amid continuing protests over the alleged NEET paper leak.According to an order issued by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (ACC), Gangwar, a 1994-batch IAS officer of the Rajasthan cadre, will replace Vineet Joshi as secretary of the department of higher education. Joshi, a 1992-batch IAS officer of the Manipur cadre, has been appointed secretary in the ministry of panchayati raj.Gangwar was serving as secretary in the department of animal husbandry and dairying under the ministry of fisheries, animal husbandry and dairying. An engineer by training, he holds a BTech in Electronics and Communication from the University of Roorkee, an MTech in Communication from IIT Delhi, and an MA in Economics from the University of Rajasthan.He has previously served in several senior positions in the Rajasthan government and the Centre, including principal secretary in Rajasthan and joint secretary in the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.The ACC also appointed T K Anil Kumar, a 1996-batch IAS officer of the Karnataka cadre and additional secretary in the Ministry of Rural Development, as secretary of the Department of School Education and Literacy.The government order did not cite any reason for the appointments. The reshuffle comes amid continuing protests by the CJP and students over the alleged irregularities in the NEET examination, with demonstrators demanding accountability and reforms in the examination system.Notably, the reshuffle went well beyond the education ministry. Katikithala Srinivas moves from housing and urban affairs to head the ministry of development of north eastern region, while Piyush Goyal shifts from secretary, ministry of mines, to secretary general of the national human rights commission; with Keshav Chandra taking over at Mines.In animal husbandry and dairying, vacated by Gangwar's move, Sushil Kumar Lohani has been posted as Officer on Special Duty before formally taking over as Secretary. Other new appointments include D. Thara at the Department of Capital Development (Housing and Urban Affairs), Chandra Bhushan Kumar at Labour and Employment, Satendra Singh at Urban Development, and Suchindra Misra at the National Commission for Minorities.The ACC also approved in-situ upgradation to Special Secretary rank, with Secretary-level pay, for four officers in their current postings: Mritunjay Kumar Narayan (Registrar General of India), Santosh Kumar Yadav (Chairman, NHAI), Rajeev Singh Thakur (Adviser, NITI Aayog), and Dilip Kumar (DG Training, Skill Development Ministry).
Article Summary: Naresh pal gangwar appointed as secretary of the department of higher education. Vineet joshi replaced as education secretary in the ministry of panchayati raj. Katikithala srinivas moves from housing and urban affairs to ministry of development of north eastern region.

Article Title: CJP protest: Delhi Metro shuts 17 stations for third day; see full list
Article Text:
17 Delhi Metro stations will be closed for entry and exit from Friday morning.

The stations that will remain closed are:

Lok Kalyan Marg

Rajiv Chowk

Patel Chowk

Ramakrishna Ashram Marg

Barakhambha Road

Supreme Court

Seva Teerth

Janpath

Mandi House

Central Secretariat

ITO

Delhi Gate

Indraprastha

Khan Market

Jor Bagh

Shivaji Stadium

Jhandewalan





NEW DELHI: The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) on Friday announced that 17 metro stations across the national capital will remain closed for entry and exit from 7.30am on Friday until further instructions, citing "security reasons".The latest advisory marks the third consecutive day of metro disruptions in central Delhi amid the ongoing Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)-led protest at Jantar Mantar.In a post on X, the DMRC said interchange facilities will remain available at Rajiv Chowk, Mandi House and Central Secretariat despite the closures.The decision comes a day after the DMRC shut 17 stations on Thursday, following the closure of 16 stations on Wednesday, when hundreds of office-goers, students and other commuters were stranded during the morning rush hour.Entry gates at Mandi House, Rajiv Chowk and Central Secretariat were reopened later on Thursday night.The repeated closures come amid heightened security across central Delhi following the July 20 'Sansad Chalo' march, which witnessed clashes between protesters and police near Parliament.Since then, authorities have maintained heavy security deployment around Jantar Mantar, Parliament and adjoining areas, with prohibitory orders under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) remaining in force across the New Delhi district.The CJP has continued its sit-in at Jantar Mantar, demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged NEET paper leak and broader reforms in the examination system.The DMRC has advised commuters to plan their journeys accordingly, adding that normal operations at the affected stations will resume only after further instructions.
Article Summary: 17 delhi metro stations will be closed for entry and exit from friday morning. The latest advisory marks the third consecutive day of metro disruptions in central delhi. The closures come amid heightened security across central delhi following the july 20 'sansad chalo' march.

Article Title: Rahul Gandhi's protest outside PM house ‘insincere’: Sonam Wangchuk’s wife
Article Text:
Gitanjali J Angmo, activist Sonam Wangchuk’swife, called “insincere” the flash protest led by CongressMP Rahul Gandhi

NEW DELHI: Activist Sonam Wangchuk 's wife, Gitanjali J Angmo, has criticised the flash protest led by Congress neta Rahul Gandhi near PM Narendra Modi's residence on July 21 as "insincere"."The protest outside the PM's house lacked sincerity. It would have been more sincere if it had been done by joining hands at Jantar Mantar. So, the public cannot be fooled anymore. This is an awakened India," Angmo told India Today in an interview."The awakened India movement has shown us and shown every political functionary that they now have to walk the talk. They cannot gaslight or be insincere anymore. They have to be sincere, otherwise, they will be thrown out by this young generation that has understood what they have to do and what they have to stand for..."Her remarks came two days after Rahul and other senior netas staged a sit-in near the PM's residence at 7 Lok Kalyan Marg over the crackdown on student protesters during their march towards Parliament on July 20. Taking on BJP, Angmo said, "I am very ashamed to say that BJP calls itself a Hindu nationalist party. Because there is nothing about them that is Hindu, and there's nothing about them that is nationalistic," she was quoted as saying."Everybody was there for a cause. That was a demonstration of what Hinduism is. Obstruction of justice, obstruction of freedom, obstruction of protest and terming youths as anti-nationals - this is not nationalism," she said.In an X post, Angmo, who is the co-founder of Himalayan Institute of Alternatives Ladakh (HIAL), said, "As an educator, I find it deeply painful to watch the image of our students being tarnished on media... The students' protest is taking place peacefully at Jantar Mantar. If there are reports of violence elsewhere in Delhi, please do not rush to attribute them to this movement without evidence." She added, "Our children deserve to be protected, not portrayed as villains. Their voices deserve to be heard, not drowned out by narratives that serve vested interests."
Article Summary: Gitanjali j angmo, activist sonam wangchuk’s wife, called “insincere” the flash protest led by congressmp rahul gandhi. Her remarks came two days after rahul and other senior netas staged a sit-in near the pm's residence at 7 lok kalyan marg.

Article Title: 400 Pakistani handles revived from Op Sindoor target NEET protests, being blocked: Delhi Police
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Delhi Police on Thursday said it had identified and blocked more than 400 Pakistani social media handles allegedly spreading misinformation and rumours amid the ongoing protests, urging people not to fall for fake content circulating online.Addressing the media, deputy commissioner of Police (DCP) Sumit Jha said the same social media accounts had also been active during Operation Sindoor and were now attempting to exploit the current situation to provoke people and distract students."We have noticed that some attempts are being made to spread misinformation on social media. So far, more than 400 Pakistani social media handles have been identified, which are taking advantage of this situation and spreading fake content and rumors. They are being blocked. These are the same social media handles which were active during Operation Sindoor as well. Their only aim is to provoke people and especially divert the attention of students. "Jha appealed to the public, particularly students and parents, not to be influenced by unverified posts, edited videos or content shared by anonymous accounts."I appeal to you that for the safety of children, do not get provoked by any fake post, edited videos or any post made by anonymous handle. Fact check any post before sharing it. Do not spread rumors and do not let any anti-social elements control your emotions."He also reiterated that the police respected citizens' right to peaceful protest."Remember that it is your right to hold a peaceful protest and Delhi Police respects this right."
Article Summary: Delhi police says it has identified and blocked more than 400 pakistani social media handles. They are allegedly spreading misinformation and rumours amid the ongoing protests. Deputy commissioner of police sumit jha urges people not to fall for fake content circulating online.

Article Title: 'In view of possible violence in the country': Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike after 26 days; PM Modi 'prays for his health'
Article Text:
Sonam Wangchuk ended his hunger strike after 26 days

NEW DELHI: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk on Friday ended his 26-day hunger strike in the presence of Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh, saying the decision followed prolonged negotiations and was taken to avoid any possibility of violence in the country.In a post on X, Wangchuk said he broke his fast in the presence of the two Union ministers and senior leaders of the Apex Body of Leh Ladakh. He said 65 MPs from different political parties had either visited him or signed letters urging him to end the fast."This was done after a long negotiation on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country. I will explain the conditions in detail in a separate video very soon. Meanwhile, I urge you all to stay very vigilant about not allowing violence of any kind anywhere," Wangchuk said. PM Modi wished the activist good health. "I urge Sonam ji to follow his routine as per the doctors' advice and regain his old weight as soon as possible. I pray to the Lord that Sonam ji remains healthy," the Prime Minister said.Meanwhile, Cockroach Janata Party, with whom Wangchuk had aligned with when he started the hunger strike, said, "We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days.Thank you, sir, for your extraordinary courage and sacrifice. By putting your own life on the line, you awakened the conscience of an entire nation. Your life is far too precious to this country," said founder Abhijeet Dipke.Wangchuk had been shifted from Delhi's Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram following directions from the Delhi High Court.Meanwhile, PM Modi also released a video message addressing students protesting over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. Calling the issue "very painful" for lakhs of students and their families, the Prime Minister said the Union Cabinet would consider stricter measures against paper leaks, including provisions for fast-track courts and harsher punishment for offenders, with the government aiming to introduce the legislation in Parliament during the ongoing Monsoon Session. He also said the government's immediate priority after the alleged leak was to ensure that nearly 22 lakh students did not lose an academic year by conducting the re-examination at the earliest.On the other hand, the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) has called to continue nationwide "peaceful protests" demanding the resignation of union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. "The Cockroach Janta Party’s peaceful protest at Jantar Mantar will continue until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns," said Dipke in the statement about Wangchuk ending his hunger strike.The Centre had reiterated its willingness to hold talks with student representatives. Union minister Jitendra Singh said the government had sent four formal proposals for discussions and that he and Union Health Minister JP Nadda would be present during the talks.However, CJP chief spokesperson Saurav Das said the party had not received any communication from the government and remained ready for talks at Jantar Mantar or a neutral venue such as the Constitution Club of India.Read more: 'More strict actions to come against paper leaks' - PM Modi announces new Bill push
Article Summary: Sonam wangchuk ended his hunger strike after 26 days in the presence of union ministers jp nadda and jitendra singh. He said 65 mps from different political parties had either visited him or signed letters urging him to end the fast. Cockroach janata party (cjp) has called to continue nationwide "peaceful protests" demanding resignation of education minister dharmendra pradhan.

Article Title: 2,500 detained, homes of 2 LeT terrorists razed in Kashmir after cop killing
Article Text:
2 LeT terrorists arrested

SRINAGAR: Security forces demolished family homes of two “wanted” Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorists and detained more than 2,500 people for questioning across Kashmir overnight in a sweeping crackdown following Wednesday’s fatal shooting of a J&K police head constable on duty in Lal Chowk market in Anantnag town.Officials said Thursday forces used controlled explosives to demolish the house where Adil Ahmad Thoker’s family lives in Guri village of Anantnag district. It was the second time the house was blown up after a similar demolition following the Pahalgam terrorist attack that killed 26 people on April 22, 2025.Forces also demolished the house of Haroon Rashid Ganaie’s family at Hassanpora Tawella in Bijbehara. Teams reached both villages around 3am and asked occupants to leave before detonating explosives. Thoker and Ganaie have been associated with LeT since 2018.Police said those detained were suspected “overground workers” accused of providing logistics, shelter, cash, transport, communications and other support to terrorists. About 700 were detained in Srinagar district, 178 in Baramulla, 200 in Budgam, 100 in Ganderbal, and several hundred more in Anantnag, Shopian and Kulgam.A police officer said the operation aimed to identify and dismantle networks providing logistical, financial, communication, transport and shelter support to terrorist organisations.LG Manoj Sinha chaired a security review Thursday and directed officials to remain “highly vigilant” and neutralise emerging threats. He also reviewed arrangements for Amarnath Yatra, suspended since July 19 because of heavy rain. More than 391,000 have visited the cave shrine in Anantnag district in the first 20 days of the annual pilgrimage.Officials said the manhunt continues for the lone terrorist who murdered 45-year-old head constable Ashiq Hussain Qureshi, a father of three from Lalpora village in central Kashmir’s Budgam.CM Omar Abdullah criticised the demolitions and detentions, calling the police action counter-productive. “People are angry that a police head constable has been killed so brutally. But when the number of arrests rises from hundreds to 2,000 or 2,500, that public anger risks being redirected against us,” he said. “So, demolishing houses or making thousands of arrests will not help improve the situation.”Peoples Conference chairman Sajad Lone and PDP president Mehbooba Mufti called the detentions “mass arrests”, saying they “look more like community punishment”.
Article Summary: 2 let terrorists arrested after fatal shooting of a j&k police head constable. More than 2,500 people detained across kashmir overnight in sweeping crackdown. Police said those detained were suspected ‘overground workers’ accused of providing logistics, shelter, cash, transport, communications and other support to terrorists.

Article Title: SC issues notice to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla against approval of Sena (UBT) MPs' merger with Shinde faction
Article Text:
SC issues notice to Om Birla (ANI images)

Moved to SC earlier

Merger alters Lok Sabha numbers

NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Wednesday issued notices to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, the Lok Sabha Secretary and six rebel Shiv Sena MPs on a plea filed by the Uddhav Balasaheb Thackeray (UBT) faction challenging the Speaker's decision to recognise the merger of the six MPs with the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena.The top court has further posted the matter for hearing after two weeks.The plea was filed by UBT MP Arvind Ganpat Sawant against the Speaker's decision, which was approved ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament. A bench comprising Justices PS Narasimha and Alok Aradhe issued notice to the respondents while refusing to stay the Speaker's order at this stage.The Shiv Sena (UBT) on Tuesday approached the Supreme Court challenging Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla's decision to approve the merger of its six MPs with the Shiv Sena led by Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde.Senior advocate Devadatt Kamat, appearing for the Uddhav Thackeray-led party, mentioned the matter before a bench comprising Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M. Pancholi, seeking an urgent hearing on Wednesday.Ahead of the Monsoon Session, Speaker Om Birla approved the merger of six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs with the Shinde-led Shiv Sena.The MPs are Sanjay Deshmukh (Yavatmal), Sanjay Jadhav (Parbhani), Sanjay Dina Patil (Mumbai North East), Nagesh Patil-Ashtikar (Hingoli), Omprakash Rajenimbalkar (Dharashiv) and Bhausaheb Wakchaure (Shirdi).With the merger, the Uddhav Thackeray-led Shiv Sena's strength in the Lok Sabha has come down to three MPs, while the Shinde-led Shiv Sena's tally has increased to 13 MPs, making it the third-largest constituent of the NDA after the BJP and the Telugu Desam Party (TDP).Reacting to the development, Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde said the six MPs joined his party to secure development for their constituencies and not for "personal gains".
Article Summary: Sc issues notice to lok sabha speaker om birla, the lok sabha secretary and six rebel shiv sena mps. Plea filed by the uddhav balasaheb thackeray (ubt) faction challenging speaker's decision to recognise the merger of the six mps with the eknath shinde-led shivsena. The top court has further posted the matter for hearing after two weeks.

Article Title: NIA arrests absconding PFI district chief in Kerala's Sreenivasan murder case
Article Text:
NIA arrested a key absconder in Kerala’s Sreenivasan murder case

NEW DELHI: National Investigation Agency (NIA) ion Thursday arrested a key absconder in Kerala’s Sreenivasan murder case, linked with the banned Popular Front of India (PFI) terrorist organisation.Muhammadali K.P, the PFI Palakkad district president, was taken into custody on Thursday morning at the IGI Airport, New Delhi, on his arrival from Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, where he had been hiding for the past four years. With this, the total number of accused arrested in the case now stands at 67.Among the 65 accused chargesheeted so far in the case, Muhammadali K.P is facing charges under relevant sections of IPC and UA (P) Act.Hailing from Palakkad district in Kerala, the wanted accused was a prominent perpetrator of the murder conspiracy and had also harboured the assailants involved in the brutal killing of Sreenivasan on April 16, 2022. NIA had declared a reward of Rs 3 lakh for information leading to his arrest and an Interpol Red Notice was also issued against him.Sreenivasan was killed by PFI cadres in pursuance of the PFI conspiracy to unleash terror with the nefarious agenda of establishing Islamic Rule in India by 2047. NIA, which had registered the case in September 2022, found during investigation that the accused involved in the case had conspired to create a communal divide through violent extremism and Jihad in the country.Investigations had further revealed that PFI was operating through various Wings and units established for the said purpose, and was using its offices, campuses, facilities and infrastructure to impart arms training to selected cadres in the guise of physical education, yoga training etc through its physical education Wing. The reporters wing was tasked with collecting information about prospective targets, while the actual terror acts were executed by the ‘service teams’ who trained in handling of weapons.
Article Summary: Nia arrested a key absconder in kerala’s sreenivasan murder case. Muhammadali k.p, the pfi palakkad district president, was taken into custody on thursday morning at the igi airport, new delhi. He had been hiding for the past four years. With this, the total number of accused arrested in the case now stands at 67.

Article Title: 2 new secretaries in education ministry, Vineet Joshi moved to panchayati raj
Article Text:
Joshi moved to panchayati raj

NEW DELHI: Amid ongoing protests over NEET-UG paper leak issue, govt Thursday named new secretaries for higher and school education departments, with incumbent Vineet Joshi, who had charge of both, moved to low-profile panchayati raj department.While animal husbandry secretary Naresh Pal Gangwar was appointed the new secretary for higher education, TK Anil Kumar will be the new school education secretary. The changes mark an overhaul of sorts in education ministry bureaucracy, as Abhishek Singh was earlier named head of the National Testing Agency , which conducts NEET, CUET and other exams.In early June, CBSE chief Rahul Singh and secretary Himanshu Gupta were transferred out of the National Testing Agency after a controversy over on-screen marking.The secretary-level appointments included 13 new department heads, including a bifurcation of ministry of housing and urban affairs. MoHUA secretary K Srinivas has been shifted to development of north eastern region (DoNER) ministry. D Thara, from Gujarat cadre, has been appointed secretary of the just-created department of capital development, while her batchmate Satendra Singh from Jharkhand will lead the department of urban development.The changes in education ministry are significant as govt has said demand for removal of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan is not under consideration, but steps will be taken to address concerns. The appointments committee of cabinet also desginated Chandra Bhushan Kumar as new labour secretary, while Keshav Chandra will be the new mines secretary. Odisha cadre IAS officer SK Lohani has been designated as animal husbandry secretary.
Article Summary: Vineet joshi, who had charge of both, moved to low-profile panchayati raj department. Animal husbandry secretary naresh pal gangwar was appointed the new secretary for higher education. Tk anil kumar will be the new school education secretary.

Article Title: 'More strict actions to come against paper leaks': PM Modi announces new Bill push in video message
Article Text:
Prime Minister assures students that strict action will be taken against paper leaks on Friday

NEW DELHI: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday announced that government will bring in more strict actions against paper leaks. In a late night video message, PM Modi said that the Union Cabinet will discuss these stringent measures on Friday and the government will try to get the Bill passed in Parliament next week.He assured that the Centre will bring in stricter measures to curb examination paper leaks, including provisions for fast-track courts and harsher punishments for offenders.His message comes amid massive nationwide protests against alleged irregularities in the NEET UG examination. "I know that paper leak is not an ordinary issue. It is very painful for lakh of students and their guardians.""Numerous effective measures have been taken in the past two and a half months since the paper leak incident. The culprits have been caught and are in jail. Our most important responsibility was to ensure that students' year is not wasted. The government used all its might to ensure that 22 lakh students could take their exams in the shortest possible time," he added.Further talking about the bill on fast track courts he added, "On the 19th July, the results were declared. I directed the departments today to establish a fast-track court. The departments presented me with the resolution late at night. This resolution, along with the provision for a fast-track court and harsh punishment, will be discussed in the cabinet tomorrow. After the suggestions of cabinet colleagues, it will be finalised, and since the second week of Parliament is starting from Monday, efforts will be made to get that bill passed in the House as soon as possible. "This after PM's remarks, at the NDA parliamentary party meeting on Tuesday, saying that the govt is working to make the exam system “foolproof”.
Article Summary: Prime minister assures students that strict action will be taken against paper leaks on friday. Pm modi said that the union cabinet will discuss these stringent measures on friday and the government will try to get the bill passed in parliament next week.

Article Title: Road crashes killed a record 1.8 lakh in 2025; 502 people daily
Article Text:
Road crashes claimed a little over 1.8 lakh lives in 2025

Death tol lrises

Road crashes claimed a little over 1.8 lakh lives in 2025, translating into 502 deaths per day, which is the maximum since govt started collating data. While the highest increase in such fatalities was reported in Uttar Pradesh -- from 24,118 in 2024 to 27,550 last year -- Punjab saw a major dip in road deaths -- from 4,759 in 2024 to 3,062 last year.Data that the road transport ministry provided to Lok Sabha on Thursday show that road crashes shot up by 5.3% to over 5.1 lakh in 2025 and fatalities increased by 3.5%. Ministry’s written reply indicates that 18 states and UTs saw a rise in road deaths in 2025 while 17 others reported a decline. Significantly, Lakshadweep has not reported any road death in the past two years.Ministry said data for West Bengal, which reported fewer number of fatalities in road crashes, have been taken from eDAR records as of Jan 2, 2026, while in the case of all others, data have been taken from eDAR records as of July 22. The e-Detailed Accident Report (eDAR) is a digital central accident database management system designed to analyse causes of crashes and devise safety measures. Officials said the overall number of fatalities may increase when the data for West Bengal is updated.Experts say the rise in road deaths despite efforts of govt to bring them down indicates that the current strategies have not proven that effective, even in states like Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh and Assam where non-profit organisations in the sector have collaborated with local govts.On Wednesday, the ministry had informed Rajya Sabha that 6,358 “black corridors” on the National Highway (NH) network were identified during 2023-25, which recorded 37,164 deaths. The maximum such black corridors were in Tamil Nadu (958), followed by UP (671) and Karnataka (607). Among districts, Viluppuram in Tamil Nadu had a maximum of 70 such corridors.As per ministry, unlike isolated black spots, these corridors are continuous stretches of NH that have recorded a higher number of road crashes that led to fatalities and grievous injuries. The identification is expected to help the authorities prioritise engineering interventions and improve road safety outcomes, officials said.
Article Summary: Road crashes claimed a little over 1.8 lakh lives in 2025, translating into 502 deaths per day. The highest increase in such fatalities was reported in uttar pradesh. Punjab saw a major dip in road deaths -- from 4,759 in 2024 to 3,062 last year.

Article Title: Fast-track courts, bill to tackle leaks: PM Modi
Article Text:
PM Modi shared a late night video message

NEW DELHI: PM Modi late Thursday announced that the Union Cabinet will on Friday discuss a bill to further strengthen the anti-cheating law to ensure stricter punishment to offenders.Hours after ordering the establishment of fast-track courts, in a recorded message posted on social media at midnight, PM said that based on the discussion a bill will be moved on Monday, when the second week of the current Parliament session begins, and govt will seek its passage at the earliest."Fast-track courts and strict punishment will be discussed in the Cabinet tomorrow and it will be given final shape after the discussion," Modi said, while underlining that govt has been working on addressing the concerns related to medical entrance exams paper leak over the last two and a half months. He said that govt had so far focused on ensuring that students did not lose a year and decided to conduct exams for 22 lakh students, using "all its might", with results also declared.PM Modi's message through the unusual mode came minutes after govt replaced the education secretary amid the ongoing protests against the NEET paper leak.Over the last two days, officials said, PMO has been engaged with the education ministry over the NEET exam, retest and K Radhakrishnan committee on NTA reforms, with over two dozen queries flowing from Seva Teerth.I know a paper leak is not an ordinary incident, it is very painful for lakhs of students and their parents... The culprits have been caught and are in jail. Our first and foremost responsibility was to ensure that the NEET aspirants did not lose an academic year. We used all resources to ensure that the re-exam was held in record time and results are out and lakhs of students are also celebrating," he said in the video message on X.The PM said more needed to be done, hinting the bill wo-uld be a step in that direction."Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth! Those who try to harm (it) will not be spared," he said in a post on X.
Article Summary: Pm modi shared a late night video message on social media. He said that govt had so far focused on ensuring that students did not lose a year and decided to conduct exams for 22 lakh students, using "all its might", with results also declared. The message came minutes after govt replaced the education secretary amid the ongoing protests.

Article Title: Opposition MPs take out march in solidarity with protesters
Article Text:
Opposition MPs stage protest against govt at Parliament in black clothes

NEW DELHI: Opposition MPs took out a 'candlelight march' Thursday to the Gandhi Smriti in solidarity with the protesting students, two days after they held a dharna outside the PM's residence and were forcibly evacuated by police.The MPs belonging to Congress, SP, TMC, Left parties, RJD, Shiv Sena and others met at Rahul Gandhi's residence. From there, they marched to the Gandhi Smriti holding their phone torches. The marchers included Rahul, K C Venugopal, Ramjilal Suman, Supriya Sule, Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, N K Premachandran, John Brittas, Sandosh Kumar, Sudhakar Singh, Misa Bharti, Arvind Sawant, Saugata Roy and Mahua Moitra.Rahul said, "We go to remember the students we lost - the children driven to take their own lives after the NEET paper leak. And we go to stand with the students who lie injured today - beaten for peacefully demanding justice and accountability. India's students are not alone. The entire opposition stands with them and their demands."Congress's P Chidambaram said, "All roads are barricaded, and hundreds of policemen are mobilised to 'secure' the roads of Delhi. What is govt afraid of? Why this sense of panic?" Earlier, Congress rubbished PM Modi's statement assuring the protesting youth. Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge said the students don't need platitudes after govt used pellet guns, shock batons and tear gas on them.
Article Summary: Opposition mps stage protest against govt at parliament in black clothes. The mps belonging to congress, sp, tmc, left parties, rjd, shiv sena and others met at rahul gandhi's residence. From there, they marched to the gandhi smriti holding their phone torches.

Article Title: IndiGo opposes more say for airport operators in airlines
Article Text:
Rahul Bhatia, promoter and MD of India’s largest carrier IndiGo

NEW DELHI: Rahul Bhatia, promoter and MD of India's largest carrier IndiGo , has opposed the proposed move to allow airport operators in India to own and run airlines too. "There is no global precedent for this. It raises massive conflict-of-interest concerns, and it is unclear how such a move would serve consumers' interests," he said at a post earnings call.Bhatia's Thursday comments come as govt is considering to dilute provisions that disallow operators of Delhi and Mumbai airports to own more than 10% stake in a scheduled airline. While the Adani Group, which runs Mumbai airport, is likely to make the first move by picking up stake in an existing airline or investing in a startup, the GMR Group, which operates Delhi's IGIA, could do so at a subsequent stage.The news has resulted in Interglobe Aviation's shares falling for two consecutive days as investors perceive the airline, which controls two-thirds of the domestic aviation market, to be a loser in case the move goes through.Senior aviation ministry officials said a country with the economic potential of India should have multiple airlines and not just a duopoly with IndiGo and Tata's Air India group.Meanwhile, the Competition Commission of India (CCI) Thursday invited public comments on a commitment proposal by IndiGo in a probe following its last Dec schedule collapse.The airline has proposed temporarily releasing domestic slots at airports. "Post such handover, the airport operator/authority may determine re-allocation of such slots/dates for routes, as deemed fit," the CCI notice says.
Article Summary: Rahul bhatia, promoter and md of india’s largest carrier indigo, has opposed the proposed move to allow airport operators in india to own and run airlines too. "it raises massive conflict-of-interest concerns, and it is unclear how such a move would serve consumers' interests," he said.

Article Title: SC cancels bail for accused Sonam Raghuvanshi in Meghalaya honeymoon murder
Article Text:
SC quashes Sonam’s bail Allows Sonam To File Fresh Bail Plea After 6 Months SC quashes Sonam’s bail Allows Sonam To File Fresh Bail Plea After 6 Months SC quashes Sonam’s bail 1

2

NEW DELHI: Supreme Court Thursday said "inadequate communication" of grounds of arrest to an accused would not automatically vitiate the arrest and quashed the bail granted by the Meghalaya high court to Sonam Raghuvanshi, who is facing trial for killing her husband during their honeymoon in the north-eastern state in May 2025. It directed her to surrender within three weeks.Rejecting her plea that she was not furnished grounds of arrest, which is mandatory, a bench of Justices M M Sundresh and P B Varale differentiated between complete non-service of grounds of arrest and inadequate communication of grounds of arrest, and said while the former would vitiate an arrest, in the latter case, the court has to examine whether prejudice was caused to the accused.Meghalaya HC granted Raghuvanshi bail on her fourth attempt on the ground that arrest documents mentioned the non-existent Section 403(1) of BNS instead of Section 103(1). Quashing the order, the apex court said non-furnishing of grounds of arrest could not be cited at a belated stage when regular bail plea was rejected on merit.The bench admitted that it was a tough decision to cancel the bail but said it had to be done as Raghuvanshi's continued enlargement at this stage might hinder trial in the case.The court allowed her to file a fresh bail plea after six months.The court noted that Raghuvanshi had expressed satisfaction regarding the reasons for her arrest. "As recorded earlier, the respondent expressed satisfaction on the reasons behind her arrest. Therefore, we are not inclined to go into the issue as to whether the case involves surrender or arrest followed by production before the court. Suffice it to state that both the courts committed an error in granting bail on the basis of the judgment rendered by this court," the bench said. It said the SC had ruled for furnishing written grounds of arrest on Nov 6, 2025, but she was arrested on June 9, 2025, before the judgment was delivered.Challenging the HC's order, solicitor general Tushar Mehta and the state's advocate general Amit Kumar pleaded that Raghuvanshi was wrongly granted bail and released on technical grounds of typographical error as the police had erroneously referred to Section 403(1) BNS instead of Section 103(1) BNS (punishment for murder).
Article Summary: Sc quashes sonam’s bail allows sonam to file fresh bail plea after 6 months. Sonam raghuvanshi is facing trial for killing her husband during their honeymoon in may 2025. The court allowed her to file a fresh bail plea after six months.
